toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Run tests with crash dump collection

data.reasoningText:
This is an MSTest project using Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP) — indicated by `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true`. To collect crash dumps, I can use the `--crashdump` option available in MTP, or the `dotnet test` equivalent `--blame-crash`.

Since this project uses MTP via `TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport`, I should use `dotnet test` with `--blame-crash` which enables crash dump collection.

Let me run the tests with crash dump collection enabled.

arguments.description: Run tests with crash dump collection

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-8e77cc0100914956815e01dc2705125d && dotnet test --blame-crash 2>&1", "description": "Run tests with crash dump collection", "initial_wait": 60}

data.content:
All 3 tests passed. The `--blame-crash` flag is active — if any test crashes the host process, a crash dump will automatically be collected in the test results directory.

**Command used:**
```
dotnet test --blame-crash
```

This leverages Microsoft.Testing.Platform's crash dump collector. If a crash occurs, the dump file path will be reported in the output.
